# Architectural-Integrity Benchmark — v0.3 baseline report

**Generated by:** `bench/architectural/build_report.py`

**Scope.** This is the *static baseline* of the Phase E benchmark: every task ships with a hand-curated "naive agent" candidate solution that models the typical first-attempt failure mode an LLM produces. The harness drives the candidate through both the Aether toolchain and a Python interpreter and classifies the outcome.

**Not in scope of this report:** running the same task corpus against live frontier models. That's Phase E.live, scheduled alongside the YC application v5 timeline.

## Headline numbers

- **Aether catch rate:** 10/10 — every naive-agent candidate that violates an architectural promise is rejected at compile or runtime boundary with a structured diagnostic.
- **Python silent-failure rate:** 10/10 — every naive-agent candidate runs to exit 0 with the wrong output (no exception, no warning).
- **Aether viability rate (correct refs):** 10/10 — the architecturally-correct reference runs cleanly under all default-on passes.
- **Python control rate (correct refs):** 10/10 — sanity check that the Python equivalent also runs cleanly.

## Honesty notes

- The "naive agent" candidates are hand-curated by the Aether team based on the most-common failure shapes seen in AI-generated code. They are not random samples from an LLM run. A live-model replication study against this corpus is Phase E.live work.
- Every Aether rejection corresponds to one of the documented diagnostic codes (see `grammar/diagnostics.md`); every Python silent failure produces a stdout that demonstrably violates the architectural promise stated in the task's README.
- The harness is wired into `scripts/run_all.py`; the headline numbers above are reproducible from a fresh clone by running `python3 -B scripts/run_all.py`.
